name: John W. Bubbles
abstract: John William Sublett (February 19, 1902 – May 18, 1986), known by his stage name John W. Bubbles, was an American tap dancer, vaudevillian, movie actor, and television performer.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Dody Goodman in "Dody in Dixieland"

quotes.text: and manage the theater and I will take care of my act. And I start up the stairs to tell Buck about it. Something says, don't tell him. Then I go on back and wait for the time for us to go on and Mr. Trick had introduced Buck and Bubbles, two boys that he met at Carnegie Hall and asked him would he give them a job in the theater, so he thought he'd bring us along. One plays the flute in 14 feet of rice pudding. Are the gentlemen there? Their names are Buck and Bubbles.
quotes.caption: Buck and Bubbles introduced at Carnegie Hall

quotes.text: you'll be out drinking and I'll come home drunk." This gag. He missed this trick. "Ladies and gentlemen, I had to be at Carnegie Hall the other night, two gentlemen asked me if I'd insist them running their recital in vaudeville so I thought I'd run them along. Are the gentlemen there? I think one of them plays a flute in four feet deep of rice pudding. Buck and Bubbles? Bring them on." Here we come. I told you how we're dressed. Okay. On. That's how we got on the act. Now we do our song and dance and we burned it.
quotes.caption: John Bubbles recalls a vaudeville-style Carnegie Hall gag

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: we used to always back choruses... we would play the music for quite a few of their acts, like Buck & Bubbles... John Bubbles and Buck Washington

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: he liked to see me dance like John Bubbles at that time, because John Bubbles was known as a great jazz singer.

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: I fell in love with the way John W. Bubbles, you know, his style of tap dancing... We became great friends.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: He introduced me to Buck and Bubbles... And we used to hang out together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
